items.headline: Peace talks between United States and Iran collapse without a deal
items.summary: The US and Iran failed to reach an agreement after marathon talks in Pakistan, raising doubts about whether they can find a lasting resolution to a six-week war that has killed thousands and disrupted energy supplies across the globe.

items.headline: US-Iran talks ended unsuccessfully
items.summary: The highest-level direct talks between the United States and Iran concluded without any agreement on April 12, 2026, in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan. With the breakdown of these historic peace talks, which lasted for nearly 21 hours, there is a risk of …

items.headline: 4 ways war in Iran has weakened United States in great power game
items.summary: Throughout the weeklong conflict, China and Russia struck a delicate balance. Both declined to give Iran, seen to a varying degree as an ally of both nations, their full-throated support or incur any real costs in the conflict

items.headline: US negotiators leaving without a peace deal with Iran
items.summary: A U.S. official said several U.S. Navy ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz; three supertankers passed through, shipping data showed.

items.headline: Israeli strikes kill at least 18 people across southern Lebanon
items.summary: Lebanon's Health Ministry says more than 2,000 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since March 2.

items.headline: Oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: Three supertankers laden with oil exit waterway amid global energy crunch, shipping data shows.

items.headline: How Iran’s Dark Fleet Is Quietly Keeping Oil Markets Afloat

items.headline: In the Gulf, states are 'paying the price' for a war they didn't start

items.headline: No retreat at Hormuz — Iran must not control the world’s energy lifeline
items.summary: A comprehensive sanctions regime including China is needed to deter Iran from weaponizing the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global trade artery.

items.headline: Iran war diverts US military and attention from Asia ahead of Trump's summit with China's leader
items.summary: President Barack Obama in 2011 declared it was time to leave behind the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan and “pivot” to Asia to counter China's rise

items.headline: Markets’ dilemma: Trust the bark or wag of oil prices
items.summary: Split signals. Surge in physical oil prices alongside a steep fall in futures underscores a stark divergence, with the gap between the two widening to historic highs

items.summary: The global energy crisis sparked by the Iran war is shaking up Canada coast to coast and could have a lasting legacy. Read more

items.headline: Russia Expands Gasoline Export Ban, Faces Major Oil Outages Due to Drone Strikes
